notes: recommendation has GC stop-the-world pauses (commit 9f3c1aa builds an unbounded per-request cache batch). product-page calls recommendation, times out, and RETRIES aggressively (retry_count explodes). The amplified retries make product-page's own outbound request rate and CPU spike, so naive reading blames product-page as the traffic source. True origin is downstream: recommendation. The classic reversed edge is product-page->recommendation (request-flow direction) instead of recommendation->product-page (causal direction).
_design_note: Paged on product-page because that is where the traffic spike and CPU saturation are observed. The spike is product-page generating extra load — but WHY is it generating extra load?
